memoryArtifacts.headline: Cotton of Etwall in 'A Genealogical and Heraldic Dictionary of the Landed Gentry of Great Britain, Vol 4 Part 1' by Bernard Burke
memoryArtifacts.description: A limited genealogy of the Cotton family of Etwall in 'A Genealogical and Heraldic Dictionary of the Landed Gentry of Great Britain' Vol. 4 Part 1, page 296; by Bernard Burke. This information is likely to be accurate, but should be supported with documentation.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Alan de Cotton於大約1290年出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾韋斯特 科頓時，他的父親Hugh de Cotton36歲，母親Elizabeth Tittenlegh27歲。他於1310年與Margaret de Acton在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1322年在英國英格蘭享年33歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alan de Cotton于大约1290年出生在英国英格兰史洛普爾韋斯特 科頓时，他的父亲Hugh de Cotton36岁，母亲Elizabeth Tittenlegh27岁。他于1310年与Margaret de Acton在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1322年在英国英格兰享年33岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Cotton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called from Old English (æt) cotum (dative plural of cot) ‘at the cottages or huts’ (or sometimes possibly from a Middle English plural, coten). Examples include Coton (Cambridgeshire, Northamptonshire, Staffordshire), Cottam (East Yorkshire, Lancashire, Nottinghamshire), and Cotham (Nottinghamshire). Particularly common in Staffordshire.
French: from a pet form of the personal name Nicolas (see Nicholas ), derived via Nicot, or of the personal name Jacques , derived via Jacot.
History: John Cotton (1584–1652) was a noted Puritan preacher, who landed at Boston, MA, from London in 1633 and became leader of the Congregationalists in America.

variants.name: Cotten
meanings: Altered form of German Kotten, a habitational name from any of several places so named in Rhineland, Westphalia, Silesia, etc., or perhaps an American shortened form of composite German surnames such as Kottenhagen, Kottenhoff, Kottenkamp (see Koth ).

variants.name: Nicholas
meanings: English (southwestern England and south Wales) and Dutch: from the personal name Nicholas (from Latin Nicolaus, from Greek Nikolaos, from nikān ‘to conquer’ + laos ‘people’). Forms with -ch- are due to hypercorrection (compare Anthony ). The name in various vernacular forms was popular among Christians throughout Europe in the Middle Ages, largely as a result of the fame of a 4th-century Lycian bishop, about whom a large number of legends grew up, and who was venerated in the Orthodox Church as well as the Catholic. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Croatian and Serbian Nikolić (see Nikolic ), Greek Papanikolaou ‘(son of) Nicholas the priest’ and Nikolopoulos . Compare Nickolas .

History: The colonial official and revolutionary patriot Robert Carter Nicholas was from a prominent VA family on both sides. His father was a British navy surgeon who emigrated c. 1700 from Lancashire, England, to Williamsburg, VA.

variants.name: Cotham
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): variant of   Cotton  .

variants.name: Cottam
meanings: English (Midlands and northern England): variant of   Cotton  .

nameMeanings.name: Alan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

en.lifeSummary: When Alan de Cotton was born about 1290, in West Cotton, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Hugh de Cotton, was 36 and his mother, Elizabeth Tittenlegh, was 27. He married Margaret de Acton in 1310, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1322, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 33.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alan de Cotton est né environ 1290, à West Cotton, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Hugh de Cotton, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Tittenlegh, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Margaret de Acton en 1310, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé en 1322, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 33 ans.

Er hat 1310 in England, United Kingdom Margaret de Acton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb 1322 im Alter von 33 Jahren in England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: Alan de Cotton(이)가 대략 1290년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 웨스트 컷에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hugh de Cotton(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Tittenlegh(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1310년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Margaret de Acton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1322년 영국 잉글랜드에서 33세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alan de Cotton родился около 1290 г. в местности вест коттон, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Hugh de Cotton было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Tittenlegh, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret de Acton в 1310 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер в 1322 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 33 лет.
